title: New RSA keypairs generated at 2048 bits
description: persona.rs:12: RsaPrivateKey::new(&mut OsRng, 2048). NIST SP 800-131A Rev. 2 (2019) projects RSA-2048 as acceptable through 2030 but recommends RSA-3072 or RSA-4096 for new key generation to provide longevity past that date. Keys generated now stay in use for the server's lifetime; if that extends past ~2030, the keys will be below current NIST recommended security levels. ActivityPub implementations (Mastodon, etc.) accept any size >= 2048, so there is no interoperability barrier to using 4096. Integrity: long-lived keys become vulnerable to factoring advances before they are rotated.
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment. 2048 matches Mastodon, 4096 marginal benefit.
